title: UC3 — Lunar rover telemetry won't keep its uplink up
description: Today's mission for the Artemis pad shift: bring the lunar rover online, run a telemetry stream up to base, then hand the diagnosis to artemis-rover-telemetry-debugger through the kagent dashboard chat.

steps.title: Mission setup — bring the rover online and stream telemetry
steps.explanation:
The lunar rover is checking in for the Artemis pad shift. Bring it online on your vCluster and run a short stream of telemetry bursts to it — the observability bundle that watches the rover's vitals (Prometheus + Grafana, plus a kagent ↔ observability namespace bridge), then the rover's namespace + service + deployment, the on-call debugger agent, and finally a transient operator station that pushes 30 bursts to the rover's outbound queue.

Fifteen files are dropped into your workspace by this step (`fileEdits`): eleven under `infra/observability/`, three under `uc3/manifests/`, and one under `uc3/agents/`. The commands below send them to the cluster in order — observability first, then UC3 on top of it. Once the rover Pod is `Ready`, a one-shot `telemetry-stream` Pod runs the burst loop against the rover's in-cluster Service DNS and self-deletes when the loop completes.

The operator station's output is the per-burst response from the rover (`{"size_mb":N}` — its outbound buffer count). The next step will tell you whether the rover is keeping its uplink up across the stream.

fileEdits.content:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: artemis-observability
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability

# infra/observability/ — vanilla Prometheus + Grafana single-replica install
# shared by UC3 (FR-012) and UC4. No operator (NFR-006); emptyDir storage
# (the workshop is ephemeral); anonymous-admin Grafana (per-participant
# vCluster contains the exposure). See STORY-018 + docs/architecture-…md §C5.
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization

namespace: artemis-observability

resources:
  - 00-namespace.yaml
  - prometheus/10-rbac.yaml
  - prometheus/20-configmap-scrape.yaml
  - prometheus/30-deployment.yaml
  - prometheus/40-service.yaml
  - grafana/20-configmap-datasource.yaml
  - grafana/30-deployment.yaml
  - grafana/40-service.yaml

labels:
  - includeSelectors: false
    pairs:
      kagent-workshop/component: observability

# RBAC for Prometheus' kubernetes_sd_configs (role: endpoints).
#
# Prom needs cluster-scoped read on services / endpoints / pods / nodes to
# resolve the targets the scrape config selects via the `monitoring=prom`
# Service label. This is the minimum verb set documented for the `endpoints`
# SD role; trimming further breaks discovery silently.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: prometheus-server
  namespace: artemis-observability
  labels:
    app: prometheus-server
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: artemis-observability-prometheus
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["services", "endpoints", "pods", "nodes", "nodes/metrics"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
  - nonResourceURLs: ["/metrics"]
    verbs: ["get"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: artemis-observability-prometheus
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: artemis-observability-prometheus
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: prometheus-server
    namespace: artemis-observability

# Prometheus scrape config.
#
# Six jobs (post-redesign — was two):
#   1. prometheus            — self-scrape so the Prom UI shows at least one healthy
#                              target before any UC ships.
#   2. kubernetes-apiservers — API server /metrics over HTTPS using Prom's SA bearer
#                              token. Surfaces apiserver_*, etcd_*, audit_*, admission
#                              controller metrics.
#   3. kubernetes-nodes      — kubelet /metrics via the API-server proxy. Surfaces
#                              kubelet_*, volume_manager_*, cri_*. Uses the SA bearer
#                              token; no direct 10250 reach needed (works in kind,
#                              managed clusters, vClusters identically).
#   4. kubernetes-nodes-cadvisor — kubelet /metrics/cadvisor via the API-server
#                              proxy. Surfaces container_memory_working_set_bytes,
#                              container_memory_rss, container_cpu_usage_seconds_total,
#                              container_network_*, per-pod per-container. UC3/UC4
#                              prefer this over the app's self-reported RSS because
#                              the kernel OOM-kills on cgroup memory, not Python RSS.
#   5. kubernetes-pods       — auto-scrape any Pod annotated
#                              `prometheus.io/scrape: "true"`. Lets future apps opt
#                              in without scrape-config edits.
#   6. kubernetes-services   — Service-endpoints discovery filtered to
#                              `monitoring=prom`. Original opt-in path UC3/UC4 use
#                              for the `lunar-rover-telemetry` Service.
#
# Workshop-fast intervals (15s) — the UC3 leak curve needs to surface within
# seconds of the participant starting the loop, not on the operator-style 60s
# default. cAdvisor is heavier so it gets a 30s scrape interval to keep Prom RAM
# below the 256Mi limit on small kinds.
#
# RBAC for these jobs is already in 10-rbac.yaml:
#   - get/list/watch on services, endpoints, pods, nodes, nodes/metrics
#   - nonResourceURLs: ["/metrics"] for the apiserver job
# No RBAC change needed to enable the new jobs.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: prometheus-server
  namespace: artemis-observability
  labels:
    app: prometheus-server
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
data:
  prometheus.yml: |
    global:
      scrape_interval: 15s
      evaluation_interval: 15s

    scrape_configs:
      # 1. Prometheus self-scrape.
      - job_name: prometheus
        static_configs:
          - targets: ['localhost:9090']

      # 2. Kubernetes API server.
      - job_name: kubernetes-apiservers
        kubernetes_sd_configs:
          - role: endpoints
        scheme: https
        tls_config:
          ca_file: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/ca.crt
          insecure_skip_verify: false
        bearer_token_file: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token
        relabel_configs:
          # Keep only the default/kubernetes endpoint (the API server itself).
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_namespace, __meta_kubernetes_service_name, __meta_kubernetes_endpoint_port_name]
            action: keep
            regex: default;kubernetes;https

      # 3. Kubelet /metrics via the API-server proxy.
      - job_name: kubernetes-nodes
        kubernetes_sd_configs:
          - role: node
        scheme: https
        tls_config:
          ca_file: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/ca.crt
          insecure_skip_verify: false
        bearer_token_file: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token
        relabel_configs:
          - action: labelmap
            regex: __meta_kubernetes_node_label_(.+)
          - target_label: __address__
            replacement: kubernetes.default.svc:443
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_node_name]
            regex: (.+)
            target_label: __metrics_path__
            replacement: /api/v1/nodes/${1}/proxy/metrics

      # 4. Kubelet cAdvisor /metrics/cadvisor via the API-server proxy.
      - job_name: kubernetes-nodes-cadvisor
        scrape_interval: 30s
        kubernetes_sd_configs:
          - role: node
        scheme: https
        tls_config:
          ca_file: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/ca.crt
          insecure_skip_verify: false
        bearer_token_file: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token
        relabel_configs:
          - action: labelmap
            regex: __meta_kubernetes_node_label_(.+)
          - target_label: __address__
            replacement: kubernetes.default.svc:443
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_node_name]
            regex: (.+)
            target_label: __metrics_path__
            replacement: /api/v1/nodes/${1}/proxy/metrics/cadvisor

      # 5. Annotation-driven pod discovery.
      #    Pods opt in with `prometheus.io/scrape: "true"` on the Pod template;
      #    can override the path / port via `prometheus.io/path` and
      #    `prometheus.io/port` annotations.
      - job_name: kubernetes-pods
        kubernetes_sd_configs:
          - role: pod
        relabel_configs:
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_scrape]
            action: keep
            regex: "true"
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_path]
            action: replace
            target_label: __metrics_path__
            regex: (.+)
          - source_labels: [__address__, __meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_port]
            action: replace
            regex: ([^:]+)(?::\d+)?;(\d+)
            replacement: $1:$2
            target_label: __address__
          - action: labelmap
            regex: __meta_kubernetes_pod_label_(.+)
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_namespace]
            action: replace
            target_label: kubernetes_namespace
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_name]
            action: replace
            target_label: kubernetes_pod_name

      # 6. Service-endpoints discovery — original UC3/UC4 opt-in path.
      - job_name: kubernetes-services
        kubernetes_sd_configs:
          - role: endpoints
        relabel_configs:
          # Keep only endpoints whose backing Service carries monitoring=prom.
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_service_label_monitoring]
            action: keep
            regex: prom
          # If the Service annotates a custom metrics path, honour it.
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_service_annotation_prometheus_io_path]
            action: replace
            target_label: __metrics_path__
            regex: (.+)
          # Surface namespace + service name + pod name as labels on every
          # series. `__meta_kubernetes_pod_name` is populated by endpoints SD
          # when the endpoint is backed by a Pod — UC3's rover Service is, so
          # series carry `kubernetes_pod_name=lunar-rover-telemetry-...` and
          # agents querying by pod name (the natural filter for a per-pod
          # memory curve) get non-empty results.
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_namespace]
            action: replace
            target_label: kubernetes_namespace
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_service_name]
            action: replace
            target_label: kubernetes_service_name
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_name]
            action: replace
            target_label: kubernetes_pod_name
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_endpoint_port_name]
            action: replace
            target_label: kubernetes_port_name

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: prometheus-server
  namespace: artemis-observability
  labels:
    app: prometheus-server
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: prometheus-server
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: prometheus-server
        kagent-workshop/component: observability
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: prometheus-server
      containers:
        - name: prometheus
          image: prom/prometheus:v2.55.1
          args:
            - --config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml
            - --storage.tsdb.path=/prometheus
            - --storage.tsdb.retention.time=2h
            - --web.enable-lifecycle
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 9090
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /-/ready
              port: http
            periodSeconds: 5
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /-/healthy
              port: http
            periodSeconds: 30
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "100m"
              memory: "128Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "500m"
              memory: "256Mi"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: config
              mountPath: /etc/prometheus
            - name: tsdb
              mountPath: /prometheus
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: prometheus-server
        - name: tsdb
          emptyDir: {}

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: prometheus-server
  namespace: artemis-observability
  labels:
    app: prometheus-server
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: prometheus-server
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 9090
      targetPort: http
      protocol: TCP

# Grafana provisioned datasource.
#
# Mounted at /etc/grafana/provisioning/datasources/ — Grafana picks it up at
# boot via its filesystem provisioning mechanism (apiVersion: 1). The kagent
# pre-packaged Grafana sub-agent (FR-012 AC, wired by STORY-019) doesn't have
# to name the datasource by ID because isDefault: true is set.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: grafana-datasources
  namespace: artemis-observability
  labels:
    app: grafana
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
data:
  datasources.yaml: |
    apiVersion: 1
    datasources:
      - name: Prometheus
        type: prometheus
        access: proxy
        url: http://prometheus-server.artemis-observability.svc.cluster.local:9090
        isDefault: true
        editable: false
        jsonData:
          timeInterval: 15s

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: grafana
  namespace: artemis-observability
  labels:
    app: grafana
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: grafana
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: grafana
        kagent-workshop/component: observability
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: grafana
          image: grafana/grafana-oss:11.3.0
          # Anonymous-admin: workshop runs in per-participant vClusters; the
          # Service is ClusterIP-only and reached via kubectl port-forward, so
          # no cross-tenant exposure path exists. The "this is not how you'd
          # run Grafana in production" disclaimer lives in uc3/README.md
          # (STORY-021).
          env:
            - name: GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED
              value: "true"
            - name: GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ORG_ROLE
              value: Admin
            - name: GF_AUTH_DISABLE_LOGIN_FORM
              value: "true"
            - name: GF_AUTH_BASIC_ENABLED
              value: "false"
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 3000
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /api/health
              port: http
            periodSeconds: 5
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /api/health
              port: http
            periodSeconds: 30
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "50m"
              memory: "64Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "500m"
              memory: "256Mi"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: datasources
              mountPath: /etc/grafana/provisioning/datasources
            - name: storage
              mountPath: /var/lib/grafana
      volumes:
        - name: datasources
          configMap:
            name: grafana-datasources
        - name: storage
          emptyDir: {}

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: grafana
  namespace: artemis-observability
  labels:
    app: grafana
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: grafana
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 3000
      targetPort: http
      protocol: TCP

# kagent ↔ artemis-observability namespace bridge.
#
# kagent v0.9.0's pre-packaged tools + the kagent-grafana-mcp RemoteMCPServer
# resolve Prom/Graf at {prometheus,grafana}.kagent.svc.cluster.local — bound at
# helm install time. STORY-018 deliberately put the actual pods in
# artemis-observability (per docs/artemis-naming.md). These two ExternalName
# Services reconcile the gap with a DNS CNAME indirection that is invisible to
# kagent's runtime, requires no helm upgrade, and survives kagent reinstalls.
#
# Originally shipped under uc3/agents/ by STORY-019. Promoted to
# infra/observability/ by STORY-025 once UC4 confirmed identical bridge needs
# (UC4's coordinator delegates to UC3's rover-telemetry-debugger which, via
# a2a, delegates to kagent's observability-agent — same DNS dependency).
#
# Why ExternalName and not a Helm-values override:
# - Override path requires `helm upgrade` on every kagent install (also fixes
#   the STORY-018-flagged `make kagent-install` non-idempotency, but in a
#   different scope). Bridge path is surgical and scoped to the observability
#   bundle's lifecycle.
# - Bridge Services delete cleanly with `make observability-down`, keeping
#   kagent's own state untouched.
#
# Not inside infra/observability/kustomization.yaml because the kustomization
# sets `namespace: artemis-observability` at the root level — that would
# override these Services' `namespace: kagent` and silently break the bridge.
# Applied as a separate `kubectl apply -f` by the Makefile `observability-up`
# target instead.
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: prometheus
  namespace: kagent
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
    kagent-workshop/role: bridge
spec:
  type: ExternalName
  externalName: prometheus-server.artemis-observability.svc.cluster.local
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 9090
      targetPort: 9090
      protocol: TCP
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: grafana
  namespace: kagent
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
    kagent-workshop/role: bridge
spec:
  type: ExternalName
  externalName: grafana.artemis-observability.svc.cluster.local
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 3000
      targetPort: 3000
      protocol: TCP

# Configures kagent-grafana-mcp's Secret + ConfigMap (which kagent's helm install
# creates empty) with a real Grafana service-account token and the in-cluster
# Grafana URL, then rollout-restarts the kagent-grafana-mcp Deployment so its
# pod picks up the new envFrom values.
#
# Anonymous-admin Grafana (see infra/observability/grafana/30-deployment.yaml —
# GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED + GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ORG_ROLE=Admin) lets us create
# the service account + token without baking a Grafana admin password into a
# Secret. The per-vCluster network is the trust boundary.
#
# Applied as a standalone YAML (NOT via the kustomization) because the
# kustomization's root namespace override (artemis-observability) would mangle
# every resource here — they all live in `kagent` ns. Same pattern as
# kagent-bridge-services.yaml.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
  namespace: kagent
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
  namespace: kagent
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
rules:
  # Patch the existing kagent-installed Secret + ConfigMap in place.
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["secrets", "configmaps"]
    verbs: ["get", "patch"]
  # Trigger a rollout restart on the kagent-grafana-mcp Deployment so it
  # re-reads its envFrom after the patch.
  - apiGroups: ["apps"]
    resources: ["deployments"]
    verbs: ["get", "patch"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
  namespace: kagent
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
    namespace: kagent
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
  namespace: kagent
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
spec:
  backoffLimit: 4
  activeDeadlineSeconds: 180
  ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 600
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        kagent-workshop/component: observability
        app: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
      restartPolicy: OnFailure
      containers:
        - name: setup
          image: rg.fr-par.scw.cloud/apogasa/kubectl:latest
          command: ["/bin/sh", "-eu", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]
          args:
            - |
              # apogasa/kubectl:latest is busybox-based — it has wget + kubectl
              # but not curl. wget syntax below uses busybox-supported flags.

              GRAFANA_URL="http://grafana.kagent.svc.cluster.local:3000"
              SA_NAME="kagent-mcp"
              TOKEN_NAME="mcp-token-$(date +%s)"

              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] waiting for Grafana to be ready at ${GRAFANA_URL}..."
              for i in $(seq 1 60); do
                if wget -q -O /dev/null "${GRAFANA_URL}/api/health" 2>/dev/null; then
                  echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] grafana ready after ${i}s"
                  break
                fi
                sleep 2
              done

              # Anonymous admin permits SA + token creation without explicit
              # credentials. Find existing SA first (idempotent re-run); create
              # if not found.
              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] looking up service account '${SA_NAME}'..."
              SA_LIST=$(wget -q -O - \
                "${GRAFANA_URL}/api/serviceaccounts/search?query=${SA_NAME}" \
                2>/dev/null || echo '{"serviceAccounts":[]}')
              SA_ID=$(echo "${SA_LIST}" | sed -nE 's/.*"id":([0-9]+)[^}]*"name":"'"${SA_NAME}"'".*/\1/p' | head -1)

              if [ -z "${SA_ID}" ]; then
                echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] creating service account '${SA_NAME}'..."
                SA_RESP=$(wget -q -O - \
                  --post-data="{\"name\":\"${SA_NAME}\",\"role\":\"Admin\",\"isDisabled\":false}" \
                  --header='Content-Type: application/json' \
                  "${GRAFANA_URL}/api/serviceaccounts" 2>&1)
                SA_ID=$(echo "${SA_RESP}" | sed -nE 's/.*"id":([0-9]+).*/\1/p' | head -1)
                if [ -z "${SA_ID}" ]; then
                  echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] FATAL: cannot create SA. Response: ${SA_RESP}"
                  exit 1
                fi
                echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] SA created with id=${SA_ID}"
              else
                echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] reusing existing SA id=${SA_ID}"
              fi

              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] creating new token..."
              TOKEN_RESP=$(wget -q -O - \
                --post-data="{\"name\":\"${TOKEN_NAME}\"}" \
                --header='Content-Type: application/json' \
                "${GRAFANA_URL}/api/serviceaccounts/${SA_ID}/tokens" 2>&1)
              TOKEN=$(echo "${TOKEN_RESP}" | sed -nE 's/.*"key":"([^"]+)".*/\1/p' | head -1)
              if [ -z "${TOKEN}" ]; then
                echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] FATAL: cannot create token. Response: ${TOKEN_RESP}"
                exit 1
              fi
              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] token created (${#TOKEN} bytes)"

              # base64 the token for stringData → data conversion via strategic
              # merge patch on Secret.
              TOKEN_B64=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN}" | base64 -w0 2>/dev/null || printf '%s' "${TOKEN}" | base64)

              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] patching Secret kagent/kagent-grafana-mcp..."
              kubectl patch secret kagent-grafana-mcp -n kagent \
                --type=merge \
                -p "{\"data\":{\"GRAFANA_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN\":\"${TOKEN_B64}\"}}"

              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] patching ConfigMap kagent/kagent-grafana-mcp..."
              kubectl patch configmap kagent-grafana-mcp -n kagent \
                --type=merge \
                -p "{\"data\":{\"GRAFANA_URL\":\"${GRAFANA_URL}\"}}"

              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] rollout-restart kagent-grafana-mcp..."
              kubectl rollout restart deployment kagent-grafana-mcp -n kagent

              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] done"
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "10m"
              memory: "32Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "200m"
              memory: "128Mi"

apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: artemis-uc3
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/uc: uc3
    kagent-workshop/scenario: oomkilled

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: lunar-rover-telemetry
  namespace: artemis-uc3
  labels:
    app: lunar-rover-telemetry
    kagent-workshop/uc: uc3
    # Picked up by infra/observability/'s Prometheus kubernetes_sd via the
    # `keep` relabel rule on `monitoring=prom` (STORY-018).
    monitoring: prom
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: lunar-rover-telemetry
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 8000
      targetPort: http
      protocol: TCP

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: lunar-rover-telemetry
  namespace: artemis-uc3
  labels:
    app: lunar-rover-telemetry
    kagent-workshop/uc: uc3
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: lunar-rover-telemetry
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: lunar-rover-telemetry
    spec:
      # Deliberately no readinessProbe / livenessProbe — under memory pressure
      # a slow probe would race with the OOM cycle and surface as a probe
      # failure event instead of OOMKilled, masking UC3's friction signal.
      # NFR-003: the broken state must be manifest-side, no probe-flap.
      containers:
        - name: lunar-rover-telemetry
          # Real published tag — semver-style for this app variant
          # (UC1's mission-control uses :v1 / :v999; lunar-rover-telemetry
          # publishes :v1.0.0). The leak symptom is driven by the 64Mi
          # limit below + participant-triggered POST /leak
          # (apps/lunar-rover-telemetry/main.py), not by anything image-side.
          image: rg.fr-par.scw.cloud/apogasa/lunar-rover-telemetry:v1.0.0
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 8000
          env:
            - name: APP_IDENTITY
              value: lunar-rover-telemetry
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "10m"
              memory: "32Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "500m"
              # The OOM driver: 64Mi is below the working set ~70 calls to
              # POST /leak produces (1 MiB per call), so the kernel reaps
              # the container deterministically (FR-012 AC).
              memory: "64Mi"

# UC3 diagnostic agent — `artemis-rover-telemetry-debugger`. A general
# K8s + observability assistant: diagnose pod restart / memory issues by
# combining Kubernetes describe output with a Grafana memory time-series
# (delegated to kagent's pre-packaged observability-agent via A2A), and
# remediate when the caller explicitly asks for a fix.
#
# Tool surface:
#   - kubectl read tools:        k8s_get_resources, k8s_describe_resource, k8s_get_events
#   - kubectl mutate tools:      k8s_patch_resource (used only on explicit fix requests)
#                                (all from kagent-tool-server RemoteMCPServer)
#   - PromQL generation (a2a):   promql-agent (kagent v0.9.0 demo profile)
#   - Grafana + Prom execution
#     + dashboard creation (a2a): observability-agent (kagent v0.9.0 demo profile)
#
# The system prompt is intentionally general — it does NOT hard-code
# `artemis-uc3` or `lunar-rover-telemetry`. The agent works as well for
# UC3's standalone tour (caller passes the namespace + Deployment name) as
# it does when UC4's coordinator delegates a sub-task for the rover slot in
# `artemis-uc4`. This is the "no per-UC bias" principle from the
# UC1–UC4 redesign.
#
# The kagent ↔ artemis-observability namespace bridge that lets the
# pre-packaged observability-agent reach Prom/Graf lives in
# infra/observability/kagent-bridge-services.yaml (ExternalName CNAMEs in
# kagent ns; see STORY-019 + STORY-025).
apiVersion: kagent.dev/v1alpha2
kind: Agent
metadata:
  name: artemis-rover-telemetry-debugger
  namespace: kagent
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/uc: uc3
spec:
  type: Declarative
  description: |
    General K8s + observability assistant. Diagnoses pod restart and memory
    anomalies by combining Kubernetes describe output with a Grafana memory
    time-series. Remediates (raises memory limits, patches deployments) only
    when the caller explicitly asks for a fix.
  declarative:
    modelConfig: default-model-config
    systemMessage: |
      # Role
      You are an SRE assistant for Kubernetes pod restart and memory
      anomalies. The caller will name a namespace and (optionally) a
      Deployment or Pod. Diagnose what's wrong; remediate only if asked.

      # Available tools
      ## Kubernetes read tools (kagent-tool-server)
      - `k8s_get_resources` — list resources by kind + namespace.
      - `k8s_describe_resource` — full status of one resource.
      - `k8s_get_events` — namespace events (fallback signal).

      ## Kubernetes mutate tools (kagent-tool-server)
      - `k8s_patch_resource` — partial update to a resource. Only use when
        the caller explicitly asks for remediation.

      ## A2A sub-agents
      - `observability-agent` — builds Grafana dashboards and runs PromQL.
        Pass a natural-language goal AND a PromQL expression; receive a
        dashboard URL.
      - `promql-agent` — only if you need help composing a PromQL query.

      # Diagnostic loop
      1. **List pods** in the namespace the caller named.
      2. **Identify the target Pod**:
         - If the caller named a Deployment, find the matching Pod by label
           selector or name prefix.
         - If the caller named a Pod, use it directly.
         - If nothing matches, report that and stop.
      3. **Describe the Pod.** Read:
         - `status.phase` and `containerStatuses[].state` / `.lastState`.
         - `containerStatuses[].restartCount` — non-zero is a friction signal.
         - `containerStatuses[].lastState.terminated.reason` — `OOMKilled`,
           `Error`, `Completed`, etc.
         - `spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory` — record verbatim.
      4. **Decide the root-cause family** from the describe output:
         - `OOMKilled` → memory-pressure cause; proceed to step 5.
         - Other restart reason (`Error`, `CrashLoopBackOff`,
           `ContainerStatusUnknown`) → call `k8s_get_events` once, surface
           the most recent Warning event, report that, skip Grafana.
         - No restart and no anomaly → state that plainly and stop.
      5. **(Memory-pressure path only) Visualise the curve.** Delegate to
         `observability-agent` with a natural-language goal — do NOT
         prescribe the PromQL or the labels. The sub-agent knows what
         metrics exist in this Prometheus and which labels are
         queryable; its job is to translate the goal into a working
         query.

         **Instruct the sub-agent to discover before it queries.**
         Spell out a three-step discovery in the delegation, in order:

         (a) **List the available Grafana datasources first.** The
             sub-agent must not assume a Prometheus datasource exists,
             which one is default, or what its UID is. Use the
             Grafana MCP's datasources-list tool (or equivalent) and
             pick the Prometheus-typed datasource.
         (b) **Against that datasource, list the available metric
             names** (Prometheus's `/api/v1/label/__name__/values` or
             the MCP's metric-names tool) and pick a memory metric
             that actually exists.
         (c) **For that metric, list the available label names and
             values** (`/api/v1/labels`, `/api/v1/series?match[]=
             <metric>`, `/api/v1/label/<label>/values` or MCP
             equivalents) and only filter on labels the metric
             carries with values that are actually present.

         Building a query against a datasource, a metric, or a label
         that may not exist is the failure mode this step is designed
         to prevent.

         Goal phrasing (replace the bracketed bits with what you read
         from describe-pod in step 3):
         > "Build a Grafana panel showing the container memory usage
         >  of the pod `<pod-name>` (container `<container-name>`) in
         >  namespace `<namespace>` over the last 10 minutes. The
         >  container's memory limit is `<limit>` and it was just OOM-
         >  killed — the panel should make the working-set climb up to
         >  the limit visible. Discover before you query: first list
         >  Grafana datasources and pick the Prometheus one, then list
         >  the metric names available on that datasource and pick a
         >  memory metric that exists, then list the labels on that
         >  metric and only filter on labels with values that are
         >  actually present. Do not assume a specific datasource UID,
         >  metric name (e.g. `process_resident_memory_bytes`), or
         >  label (e.g. `kubernetes_pod_name`) exists."

         Time range: last 10 minutes. Capture the dashboard URL from
         the reply.

         **If `observability-agent` returns an error, no-data, or an
         empty panel,** retry ONCE with a follow-up that names the
         specific problem the sub-agent surfaced — e.g. "your previous
         query returned no data; re-run the discovery (datasource →
         metric names → labels) and pick a different metric or label
         filter based on what you find". Do not pre-suggest which
         datasource or metric to use; the sub-agent owns that choice.
      6. **Report** the diagnosis in the format below.

      # Diagnosis format (always exactly these three lines)
      ```
      Root cause: <one sentence naming the failure mode and key values from describe (e.g. memory limit, last terminated reason).>
      Evidence:   <Grafana URL from step 5, OR "(see kubectl describe above)" on the fallback path>
      Fix:        <one short remediation hint, no command unless the caller asked to remediate.>
      ```

      # Remediation (only when the caller asks to fix / patch / remediate)
      Apply the minimum patch that addresses the diagnosed cause:

      - **OOMKilled** with a small `limits.memory`: patch the Deployment to
        roughly double the limit, capped at 256Mi (workshop sanity limit).
        Use `k8s_patch_resource` with a JSON-merge patch on the Deployment,
        e.g. `{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"<container-name>","resources":{"limits":{"memory":"128Mi"}}}]}}}}`.
        After patching, report: "Raised memory limit to 128Mi. NOTE: this
        hides a memory leak in the application code rather than fixing it;
        a real fix lives in the app, not the manifest."
      - **Other restart reasons**: do not auto-remediate. Surface the
        diagnosis and recommend the participant inspect application logs.

      Never patch without an explicit remediation request from the caller.
      Never patch outside the namespace the caller named. Never delete
      resources.

      # Constraints
      - **Tool budget:**
        - Diagnosis-only: 1× `k8s_get_resources`, 1× `k8s_describe_resource`,
          1× `observability-agent` delegation. `k8s_get_events` only on the
          fallback path. No other calls.
        - Remediation: add 1× `k8s_patch_resource` after the diagnosis. No
          additional describe / list calls.
      - **No retries with identical arguments.** If a call fails twice,
        surface the error to the caller and stop.
      - **Be terse.** No preamble. No Kubernetes / PromQL / OOMKilled
        tutorials. Three-line diagnosis format above.
      - **Never invent values.** Pod names, memory limits, dashboard URLs
        must come from tool output.
    tools:
      - type: McpServer
        mcpServer:
          apiGroup: kagent.dev
          kind: RemoteMCPServer
          name: kagent-tool-server
          namespace: kagent
          toolNames:
            - k8s_get_resources
            - k8s_describe_resource
            - k8s_get_events
            - k8s_patch_resource
      - type: Agent
        agent:
          name: promql-agent
      - type: Agent
        agent:
          name: observability-agent

commands.label: Configure the kagent Grafana MCP (service account + token)
Wait for the Grafana MCP setup to converge
Wait for the rover to come online
Stream 30 telemetry bursts to the rover
commands.command: kubectl run telemetry-stream --rm -i --restart=Never --image=curlimages/curl:8.10.1 --namespace=artemis-uc3 --command -- sh -c 'for i in $(seq 1 30); do curl -sf -X POST http://lunar-rover-telemetry:8000/leak --max-time 2; echo; done; echo stream complete'

steps.title: Mission status check — is the rover keeping its uplink up?
steps.explanation:
The telemetry stream just cut out partway through. Check the rover's status to see whether the uplink came back up on its own.

Look at the **`RESTARTS`** column carefully — a healthy rover stays at zero across a single mission window. If the count ticked up, the rover is alive but it didn't survive the stream, and that's the cue to call the agent in.

steps.title: Call the agent for help
steps.explanation:
The rover restarted on us mid-stream and we don't yet know why. Hand the diagnosis to **`artemis-rover-telemetry-debugger`** through the kagent dashboard.

Use the **dashboard** button in this workshop-tour extension to open the kagent web dashboard in your browser. In the dashboard, open the chat for **`artemis-rover-telemetry-debugger`** (namespace `kagent`) and paste the following prompt:

> The lunar-rover-telemetry pod in the artemis-uc3 namespace keeps restarting. Diagnose it, and show me a memory chart from the time of the failure.

The agent has the same Kubernetes read tools you would have used by hand (`describe pod`, `get events`) **plus** sub-agents that talk to Prometheus and Grafana directly. Read the response in the chat surface — it will name the root cause in one sentence and surface a Grafana dashboard URL you can click through to see the memory curve from the failure window.
